text: When you work with APIs, your application communicates with a server through HTTP requests like GET and POST. The server often returns data in JSON, a structured text format that is easy for programs to read. Many APIs require authentication, usually through an API key in the request header, to ensure only authorised users can access the data. You also need to handle rate limits, which restrict how many requests you can make within a certain time frame, and you should include error handling to manage server issues or invalid input gracefully.
